summary: PowerShell script embedded in music_source.js as an array of single-quoted JS strings joined with '\n' (rather than a JS template literal).
decision: PowerShell uses backticks (`) as its own escape character (e.g., `t for tab) and dollar-sigils ($var). JS template literals would interpret these, requiring extensive JS-side escaping that risks introducing transcription bugs. Using an array of single-quoted JS strings joined with '\n' lets PowerShell text pass through verbatim: each line is a complete PS statement, JS sees only ordinary string literals, and the resulting PS_SCRIPT constant is a faithful multi-line PowerShell program. Format-string tab uses PS's own backtick-t ("{0}`t{1}" -f $title, $artist) which prints a literal tab character.
nested_equivalent: Overseer would surface this as a code-style judgment call; Coordinator would approve since it directly serves correctness and the dispatch briefing flagged escape pattern choice as an inline-deviation candidate.
contract_impact: None. PS_SCRIPT is internal to S5; the C-S4-S5 surface (tab-delimited stdout, resolve-null on empty) is unchanged.
related: dispatch briefing 'Critical note': inline deviations expected for PS escape pattern
probe-windows-nowplaying Q2 (AsTask shim vs Status-poll): chose AsTask shim for ~15 fewer lines and simpler semantics
